description: Two concurrent POST /contexts requests with the same (agent_id, Idempotency-Key) and the same content_hash. The registry's atomic-record requirement (RFC-ACDP-0003 §6.2.1 step 4) bounds the set of acceptable observable outcomes. This fixture documents the tolerated race outcomes — implementations MUST exhibit one of them; any other outcome is a conformance failure.

input.concurrency: Two requests submitted at the same time, both carrying Idempotency-Key: idem-key-RACE and the same body (content_hash sha256:H1).

tolerated_outcomes.name: serialized: one wins, the other replays
tolerated_outcomes.notes: The losing request observed the durable record written by the winner and short-circuited per §6.2.1 step 2 (same content_hash → 200).

tolerated_outcomes.name: both committed: identical ctx_id (only safe if registry uses content-derived idempotency-record primary key)
tolerated_outcomes.notes: Both requests fully validated and one of them deduplicated to a single stored body via primary-key constraint on (agent_id, Idempotency-Key, content_hash). Acceptable only if the resulting ctx_id is identical across both responses.

non_conformant_outcomes.name: two distinct ctx_ids minted
non_conformant_outcomes.reason: Two ctx_ids for the same (agent_id, Idempotency-Key) violates the contract idempotency exists to enforce. This is the failure mode the atomicity requirement in §6.2.1 step 4 prevents.

non_conformant_outcomes.name: duplicate_publish on identical content
non_conformant_outcomes.reason: duplicate_publish is reserved for same-key-different-hash collisions (idem-003). Returning it for same-key-same-hash leaks the race condition to the producer as a programming error when the producer did nothing wrong.

implementation_note: Black-box testing of concurrent races is non-deterministic; implementers SHOULD exercise this path with a stress harness that submits N≥100 paired publishes and asserts that the (agent_id, Idempotency-Key) → ctx_id mapping has cardinality 1 across the entire run. The fixture pins the wire-observable contract; the harness pins the storage-level invariant.
